$comment: Capability token — scoped, expiring, logged authorization for a boundary-crossing action. Humans hold the keys; an agent that lacks a token for a gated action refuses and surfaces, it does not improvise.

issued_by.description: human principal — tokens are never machine-issued

action.description: ONE action, e.g. billing.issue_refund — no wildcards

target.description: the specific resource/entity; 'any' is not a value

single_use.description: default true; multi-use tokens require a documented why

why.description: the human's stated reason — copied into every provenance record this token authorizes

invariants: no token => refuse and surface; the refusal itself is logged as a provenance record with gate.mode=forbid
token use emits a provenance record referencing token_id; issuance and revocation are provenance records too
scope is one action on one target — a token that authorizes a category is a credential, not a capability
